title: Read docs before guessing config keys
description: Spent 40 minutes debugging a config key that didn't exist. The product uses strict validation and crashes on unknown keys. The docs had the answer on page one of the configuration reference.
context: Setting up a new gateway / framework / tool whose config is validated strictly.
resolution: Searched the local docs MCP for the key; found the correct name; the crash went away in 10 seconds.
prevention: Before writing or editing any config block: open the configuration-reference page (or docs MCP) and confirm every key exists. Never paste config snippets from chat history without verifying them against current docs.
trigger: About to write or modify a config file for a tool I have not used today
